result.title: "The inflection point for inference has arrived."
result.description:
"The inflection point for inference has arrived." — Jensen Huang, Founder & CEO of NVIDIA
 
We’ve officially crossed a new milestone in the inference era — where widespread adoption of AI shifts from learning to doing.

The breakthrough: extreme codesign across hardware and software driving down cost per token.

Lower cost → more inference
More inference → more users and applications
More users and applications → exponential AI revenue growth

🔗 https://nvda.ws/4bYSCij

result.title: Why Do Performance Benchmarks Matter?
result.description:
Delivered performance, not peak chip specifications, drives AI factory productivity.

Rigorous benchmarks are the only way to see past the noise. In MLPerf Inference v6.0, NVIDIA extreme co-design delivered the highest token output across the broadest range of models and scenarios. 

Maximizing token output drives down token cost and maximizes AI factory productivity.

Read the blog post to dive into the details: https://nvda.ws/48kdWOc

result.title: NVIDIA GTC Studio with Insights from Schneider Electric
result.description: Pankaj Sharma, Executive Vice President of Software and Services at Schneider Electric, outlines the challenges of scaling AI infrastructure, emphasizing energy as the foundation of the five-layer AI cake. As AI adoption accelerates, he points to growing pressure on power, grid capacity, and sustainable energy—while highlighting the opportunity to use AI to optimize and expand energy systems for the next generation of AI.

result.title: NVIDIA GTC Studio with Insights from Vertiv
result.description: Scott Armul, Chief Product and Technology Officer at Vertiv, explains how Vertiv meets AI infrastructure demands at scale—delivering the power and cooling systems behind modern data centers and developing the solutions to streamline the design and deployment of next-generation AI factories.

result.title: Healthcare Pioneers and Their Breakthroughs for a Healthier Future
result.description:
For generations, doctors and scientists have dedicated their lives to understanding and decoding disease. They uncovered how cells work, how genes are structured, and how life itself functions. Now, accelerated computing is helping extend their vision for a healthier humanity, helping us understand the language of biology, design and run digital labs, connect scientists to AI agents, and robots that work side-by-side with doctors and nurses 24/7. 

Follow us:
X: https://nvda.ws/4hi2sxB
LinkedIn: https://nvda.ws/4eSlE3B
Subscribe to the NVIDIA AI Healthcare Newsletter: https://nvda.ws/4eS1F54
